Title: Stress Responsiveness of Arabidopsis Trehalose Biosynthesis Genes

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate to trehalose-6-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in response to abiotic stresses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS2 transcript is induced by multiple abiotic stresses in root, suggesting contribution to trehalose/trehalose-6-phosphate stress responsiveness.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: induced in root by all abiotic stresses under study; later induction around 12 h at lower levels (3–6-fold)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold; osmotic; salt; drought; genotoxic; oxidative; UV-B; wounding; heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS2 is one of the Arabidopsis trehalose biosynthesis genes responsive to abiotic stress. 2. In roots, AtTPS2 is induced by all abiotic stresses examined. 3. Induction is delayed relative to AtTPS3, occurring around 12 h and at lower magnitude, supporting a stress-responsive role in trehalose/T6P metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an invited review based on in silico expression analysis; enzymatic activity was noted as unproven for many Arabidopsis trehalose biosynthetic genes.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential candidate for engineering abiotic stress-responsive trehalose metabolism, but direct metabolic and enzymatic validation is needed.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate to trehalose-6-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in response to abiotic and wound/UV-B responses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS3 shows strong and dynamic stress-responsive transcript changes in root and shoot, indicating involvement in trehalose/trehalose-6-phosphate responses to multiple stresses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root, induced by all abiotic stresses, early within 30 min and strongly (16–48-fold); under salt transiently induced then repressed after 3 h; in shoot induced transiently by osmotic and salt stress early, repressed by cold after 12 h, repressed during UV-B and heat, induced by wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and shoot stress-responsive transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold; osmotic; salt; drought; genotoxic; oxidative; UV-B; wounding; heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS3 is among the most strongly stress-responsive trehalose biosynthesis genes. 2. In roots it is induced rapidly within 30 min by all abiotic stresses tested, reaching 16–48-fold. 3. In shoots its expression is dynamic, with early induction under osmotic and salt stress but repression under prolonged cold, UV-B, and heat, and induction after wounding.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: This is an invited review based on expression profiling; enzymatic activity not proven for many genes discussed.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential marker or engineering target for stress-responsive trehalose/T6P regulation, but direct functional validation is required.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate to trehalose-6-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS4 is moderately induced by several abiotic stresses in root and weakly repressed by oxidative stress in shoot, suggesting stress-dependent regulation of trehalose/T6P metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root induced after 24 h of osmotic, salt, drought, UV-B, wounding and heat stress; in shoot weakly repressed after 6 h oxidative treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and shoot stress-responsive transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: osmotic; salt; drought; UV-B; wounding; heat; oxidative stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS4 responds to multiple abiotic and damage-related stresses. 2. Root transcripts increase mainly after prolonged treatment (24 h) under osmotic, salt, drought, UV-B, wounding, and heat conditions. 3. In shoots it is weakly repressed by oxidative stress, indicating tissue- and stress-dependent regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression evidence only; enzymatic activity not established in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for tuning late stress-responsive trehalose metabolism after direct validation.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts UDP-glucose and glucose-6-phosphate to trehalose-6-phosphate.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS6 is slightly induced in roots by osmotic and salt stress, suggesting a minor stress-responsive role in trehalose/T6P metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: slightly induced in root from 3 to 12 h by osmotic (3-fold) and salt (3.5-fold) stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS6 shows limited but detectable stress responsiveness. 2. Root transcript levels increase modestly from 3 to 12 h under osmotic and salt stress. 3. The data support a possible minor contribution to trehalose/T6P stress adaptation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Expression evidence only; enzymatic activity not established in this review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Lower-priority candidate for engineering compared with more strongly responsive TPS genes.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS8 is transiently induced in shoots by cold stress and induced during heat stress, indicating stress-responsive association with trehalose pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot transiently induced early within 1 h of cold stress (12-fold); induced during heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS8 transcripts are stress responsive in shoots. 2. Expression rises rapidly during early cold treatment and is also induced by heat stress. 3. The paper links AtTPS8 with stress-associated trehalose pathway regulation, but catalytic function is not demonstrated.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic activity not proven; therefore enzyme assignment remains uncertain.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stress marker within the trehalose pathway gene family pending biochemical validation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS10 is induced in shoots after prolonged cold stress, suggesting participation in stress-responsive trehalose pathway regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot induced 6–28 fold after 12 h of cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS10 is induced in shoots during cold stress. 2. Induction is observed after 12 h of treatment. 3. The review associates AtTPS10 with trehalose pathway stress responses without direct biochemical proof.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible cold-responsive candidate for further functional testing.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPS11 is induced by cold and weakly regulated by drought and salt in shoots, indicating stress-responsive association with trehalose pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot induced 6–28 fold after 12 h cold stress; weakly repressed after 24 h salt stress; weakly induced within 3 h drought treatment
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS11 responds to several abiotic stresses in shoots. 2. It is induced under prolonged cold treatment and weakly induced early by drought. 3. Salt treatment causes weak repression later, highlighting dynamic stress regulation.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for studying shoot-specific stress responses in trehalose pathway gene family.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPA is repressed in shoots during osmotic stress, indicating stress-dependent regulation of trehalose production capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot repressed 14-fold after 12 h osmotic stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPA transcript abundance decreases strongly under osmotic stress in shoots. 2. This indicates that not all TPP genes are induced under stress. 3. The result supports differential regulation within the Arabidopsis trehalose phosphatase family.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inferred enzyme function from TPP family membership; this review reports transcript behavior, not direct enzyme assays for this gene.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential negative marker for osmotic stress-associated trehalose biosynthesis regulation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPB is repressed by cold stress in roots and repressed during UV-B treatment in shoots, indicating negative stress regulation of trehalose-producing capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root repressed 8-fold after 12 h cold stress; in shoot repressed during UV-B treatment
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and shoot stress-responsive transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPB is negatively regulated under selected stresses. 2. Root transcripts decline markedly under cold treatment. 3. Shoot transcripts are also repressed by UV-B, supporting differential TPP family behavior during stress.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Inferred enzyme function from TPP family membership; direct catalytic validation not shown here.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential candidate for studying stress-specific repression within trehalose synthesis pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic and biotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPD is one of the most strongly stress-responsive TPP genes, highly induced by salt and other stresses and implicated in both abiotic and biotic stress-associated trehalose metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root induced by 6 h cold (3-fold), osmotic (4.3-fold), and especially salt stress (32-34.3-fold); in shoot induced early by cold (6-fold), induced with AtTPS3 early under salt then repressed strongly later, weakly induced by drought, induced up to 15-fold by UV-B, repressed by heat; induced over 160-fold by Pseudomonas syringae infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and shoot stress-responsive transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold; osmotic; salt; drought; UV-B; heat; Pseudomonas syringae infiltration
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPD is a major stress-responsive member of the Arabidopsis TPP family. 2. It is strongly induced in roots by salt stress and also induced by cold and osmotic stress. 3. In shoots it shows dynamic early induction and later repression under some stresses, is induced by UV-B and drought, repressed by heat, and is also highly induced by Pseudomonas syringae infiltration.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review highlights AtTPPD as one of the most responsive trehalose biosynthesis genes; evidence is transcriptomic rather than direct metabolite or enzyme assay. Biotic stress response noted from unpublished data reference.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising candidate for engineering stress-responsive trehalose metabolism, particularly for salt stress, but requires direct metabolic validation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPE is induced in shoots during cold stress, suggesting stress-responsive trehalose phosphatase involvement.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot induced 6–28 fold after 12 h cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPE is induced in shoots after prolonged cold treatment. 2. The data support a cold-responsive role for this TPP family gene. 3. Functional assignment here is based on family annotation and expression behavior.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not provided in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for further testing in cold-responsive trehalose engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPF is induced in roots during cold stress, suggesting cold-responsive regulation of trehalose phosphatase capacity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root induced 8-fold after 12 h cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPF is induced by cold stress in roots. 2. The induction reaches about 8-fold after 12 h. 3. This supports a possible role in root trehalose biosynthesis under cold conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not provided in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Possible root/cold-responsive candidate for future validation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPG is induced by salt stress in roots, by cold stress in shoots, and together with AtTPS3/AtTPPI after wounding, suggesting broad stress-responsive regulation of trehalose production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in root induced 11-fold after 24 h salt stress; in shoot induced 6–28 fold after 12 h cold stress and induced following wounding
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: root and shoot stress-responsive transcript accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPG is stress responsive in both roots and shoots. 2. It is induced by salt stress in roots and by cold stress in shoots. 3. It also increases after wounding, supporting a broader role in stress-associated trehalose metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not provided in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate for multi-stress trehalose engineering after direct functional confirmation.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: putative role in abiotic stress response
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: AtTPPI shows context-dependent regulation in shoots, including induction by cold, salt, drought and wounding, and repression by UV-B and heat, indicating dynamic control of trehalose phosphatase capacity under stress.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: in silico microarray expression analysis using Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: in shoot induced after 12 h cold stress, weakly induced after 12 h salt stress and within 3 h drought treatment, induced following wounding, repressed during UV-B and heat treatment
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: cold; salt; drought; wounding; UV-B; heat stress
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: microarray data from Botany Array Resource
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPPI is dynamically regulated by multiple stresses in shoots. 2. It is induced by cold and modestly by salt and drought, and also increases after wounding. 3. UV-B and heat repress AtTPPI, showing opposite responses depending on stress type.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzymatic validation not provided in the review.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for context-specific modulation of trehalose metabolism under shoot stress conditions.
